title: CSV to JSON CLI Converter
summary: A Python command-line tool that converts CSV files into JSON format, with robust error handling and data validation.

user_stories.title: User can run the CLI tool with a CSV file
user_stories.description: As a user, I want to run the CLI tool by providing a CSV file so that it can process the file and output JSON.
acceptance_criteria: The tool must accept a CSV file path as a command-line argument
The tool must display a usage message if no file is provided
The tool must exit gracefully if the file does not exist

user_stories.title: User can specify the output JSON file
user_stories.description: As a user, I want to specify where the JSON output should be saved so that I can control the destination.
acceptance_criteria: The tool must allow the user to provide an output file path via a command-line flag
If no output file is specified, the tool must default to a standard name (e.g., 'output.json')
The tool must handle file write errors gracefully

user_stories.title: User can validate CSV data before conversion
user_stories.description: As a user, I want to validate the CSV data for consistency and correctness before converting to JSON.
acceptance_criteria: The tool must check for empty or missing fields in the CSV
The tool must validate that all rows have the same number of columns
The tool must report any validation errors to the user

user_stories.title: User receives detailed error messages
user_stories.description: As a user, I want to receive clear and helpful error messages when something goes wrong during processing.
acceptance_criteria: The tool must log detailed error messages to the console
The tool must include line numbers for CSV parsing errors
The tool must not crash unexpectedly on invalid input

user_stories.title: User can view the converted JSON output
user_stories.description: As a user, I want to see the converted JSON either in the console or in a file so that I can review the results.
acceptance_criteria: The tool must print the JSON output to the console if no output file is specified
The tool must write the JSON to the specified output file
The JSON must be properly formatted and valid

non_functional_requirements.requirement: The tool must process large CSV files efficiently without excessive memory usage
The CLI must provide clear and concise help messages
The tool must handle unexpected input and system errors gracefully
The code must be well-documented and follow PEP8 style guidelines

constraints: The tool must be written in Python 3.7+
The tool must be compatible with Linux, macOS, and Windows operating systems
Third-party libraries must be minimal and well-maintained
assumptions: Users are familiar with the command line interface
CSV files will not contain complex nested structures
The tool will not support binary or non-ASCII characters in CSV
out_of_scope: Cloud-based file upload or storage integration
Real-time data streaming from CSV to JSON
tech_stack_preferences: Standard library for CSV and JSON processing
Logging module for error and debug messages
